calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate Arbitration Award Of Respondent, Yurpal, Inc.
Off Calendar Mar-19-2018 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Matthew Giampaoli, Esq. of Collinsworth Specht Calkins & Giampaoli, LLC, T:925.400.9575, for respondent/moving party;
Trevor A. Caudle, Esq. of Trevor Caudle Law Practice, T:415.260.0947, for petitioner/opposing party, by Court Call.

RULING: The tentative ruling was contested and argued. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

RESPONDENT YURPAL INC.'s Motion To Vacate Arbitration Award is DENIED. Yurpal failed to establish that the arbitrator's denial of Yurpal's discovery request for "certified payroll for Rich Flood" constituted either misconduct or a refusal to hear evidence material to the controversy. Even if the arbitrator's decision was legally or factually erroneous, the court lacks the authority to vacate the award absent proof of misconduct or refusal to hear evidence. Yurpal presents no evidence showing that the arbitrator denied its remaining discovery requests. Yurpal's argument that Surge failed to produce documents requested and never provided Mr. Flood's affidavit does not show that the arbitrator committed misconduct or refused to hear material evidence. Surge's request for attorneys' fees is denied because it failed to identify any authority providing for an award of fees.

Counsel for petitioner is to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and submit it in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR#8600, email: torreanom@aol.com; mtorreano@sftc.org.

Notice Of Petition And Petition To Confirm Final Award Of Arbitrator; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Trevor A. Caudle, Esq.; Notice Of Payment Of Court Reporter Fee
Off Calendar Mar-19-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Trevor A. Caudle, Esq. of Trevor Caudle Law Practice, T:415.260.0947, for petitioner/moving party, by Court Call;
Matthew Giampaoli, Esq. of Collinsworth Specht Calkins & Giampaoli, LLC, T:925.400.9575, for respondent/opposing party.

RULING: The tentative ruling was contested and argued. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PETITIONER SURGE LLC's Petition To Confirm Final Award Of Arbitrator is GRANTED. Surge is entitled to an order confirming the final award because there are no grounds to either vacate or correct that award.

Counsel for petitioner is to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and submit it in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR#8600, email: torreanom@aol.com; mtorreano@sftc.org.
